REQUEST TO STRIKE PETITIONER'S OPPOSITION TO RESPONDENT'S MOTION TO VACATE THE ARBITRATION AWARD AND ORDER THE MATTER BACK TO ARBITRATION FILED BY RESPONDENT DIVERSIFIED PRODUCT INDUSTRIES, LTD.
MINI-MINUTES FOR JUN-01-2010 9:30 AM FOR DEPT 302

LAW AND MOTION 302, RESPONDENT DIVERSIFIED PRODUCT INDUSTRIES MOTION TO VACATE ARBITRATION AWARD AND ORDER THE MATTER BACK TO ARBITRATION. ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: MOTION IS GRANTED. RELIEF GRANTED UNDER THE COURT'S EQUITABLE POWERS. RESPONDENT'S MISTAKE WAS EXTRINSIC AND IT MOVED DILIGENTLY TO SEEK RELIEF UPON ITS PERSONAL RECEIPT OF THE AWARD. (SEE DEMELLO VS. SOUZA (1973) 36 CAL.APP. 3RD 79, 84-86. THE COURT ADDS: ARBITRATION TO BE COMPLETED WITHIN 6 MONTHS FROM TODAY'S DATE. PREVIOUSLY AGREED TO ARBITRATOR ALLAN HARRIS IS TO BE THE ARBITRATOR. ALSO, BAY CITIES MAY BRING MOTION FOR ATTORNEYS FEES AND COSTS. (DEFENSE COUNSEL TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER.) JUDGE: CHARLOTTE WALTER WOOLARD, REPORTER: BRENDA FIELDS, CSR #7235

calendar.matters:
Notice And Motion To Be Relieved As Counsel
Off Calendar Sep-07-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
STEVEN T. LOWE/LOWE LAW, A PROF. CORP.'S MOTION TO BE RELIEVED AS COUNSEL COUNSEL (FOR DEFENDANT DIVERSIFIED PRODUCT INDUSTRIES, LTD.).

APPEARANCES: Steven T. Lowe, Esq. of Lowe Law, P.C. 310/477-5811 moving to be relieved as counsel for deft (appearing via CourtCall); no other appearances.

RULING: The Court adopted its tentative ruling - granted. No opposition filed. Order signed in open court (counsel, appearing telephonically, to receive copy in mail). (302/CWW) JUDGE: CHARLOTTE WALTER WOOLARD, REPORTER: KENT GUBBINE, CSR #5797

Ntc And Motion To Confirm Arbitratrion Award
Off Calendar Jun-14-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: PETITIONER BAY CITIES PAVING & GRADING, INC. Motion to Confirm Arbitration Award. Appearances: Walter S. Chen, Esq. of Rogers Joseph O'Donnell, appearing for Petitioner and Moving party; Steven T. Lowe, Lowe Law (310) 477-5811 appearing for Respondent and opposing party. Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: Motion is denied. (302/CWW) Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard; Court Reporter: Brenda Fields, CSR# 7235

Ntc And Motion To Vacate Arbitration Award
Off Calendar Jun-14-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: RESPONDENT DIVERSIFIED PRODUCT INDUSTRIES Motion to Vacate Arbitration Award and Order the matter back to arbitration. Appearances: Walter S. Chen, Esq. of Rogers Joseph O'Donnell, appearing for Petitioner and opposing party; Steven T. Lowe, Lowe Law (310) 477-5811 appearing for Respondent and moving party. Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: Motion is granted. Relief granted under the court's equitable powers. Respondent's mistake was extrinsic and it moved diligently to seek relief upon its personal receipt of the award. (See Demello vs. Souza (1973) 36 Cal.App. 3rd 79, 84-86. The Court adds: Arbitration to be completed within 6 months from today's date. Previously agreed to arbitrator Allan Harris is to be the arbitrator. Also, Bay Cities may bring motion for attorneys fees and costs. (Defense counsel to prepare a form of order.) (302/CWW) Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard; Court Reporter: Brenda Fields, CSR# 7235

Ntc And Motion To Confirm Arbitratrion Award
